devanagari: ऋचीकजमदग्निपरशुरामचरितं सहस्रार्जुन वधश्च । श्रीशुक उवाच । ऐलस्य च उर्वशीगर्भात् षडासन्नात्मजा नृप । आयुः श्रुतायुः सत्यायू रयोऽथ विजयो ...
english_devnagari: ṛcīkajamadagniparaśurāmacaritaṃ sahasrārjuna vadhaśca śrīśuka uvāca ailasya ca urvaśīgarbhāt ṣaḍāsannātmajā nṛpa āyuḥ śrutāyuḥ satyāyū rayo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Purūravas, the son of Ilā, had six sons by Ūrvaśī, Oh King. They were Āyu, Śrutāyu, Satyāyu, Raya, Vijaya and Jaya.

devanagari: श्रुतायोर्वसुमान्पुत्रः सत्यायोश्च श्रुतञ्जयः । रयस्य सुत एकश्च जयस्य तनयोऽमितः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutāyorvasumānputraḥ satyāyośca śrutañjayaḥ rayasya suta ekaśca jayasya tanayo
translation: Śrutāyu’s son was Vasumān; Śrutañjaya was Satya’s son. Raya’s son was Eka, and Jaya’s son was Amita.

devanagari: भीमस्तु विजयस्याथ काञ्चनो होत्रकस्ततः । तस्य जह्नुः सुतो गंगां गण्डूषीकृत्य योऽपिबत् । जह्नोस्तु पूरुस्तस्याथ बलाकश्चात्मजोऽजकः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhīmastu vijayasyātha kāñcano hotrakastataḥ tasya jahnuḥ suto gaṃgāṃ gaṇḍūṣīkṛtya yo jahnostu pūrustasyātha balākaścātmajo
translation: Vijaya had a son called Bhīma whose son was Kañcana from whom was born Hotraka. His son was Jahnu who took all the waters of the Gaṅgā in his folded palms, and drank up the whole river. Jahnu’s son was Puru, whose son was Balāka and his son was Ajaka.

devanagari: ततः कुशः कुशस्यापि कुशाम्बुस्तनयो वसुः । कुशनाभश्च चत्वारो गाधिरासीत् कुशाम्बुजः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ kuśaḥ kuśasyāpi kuśāmbustanayo vasuḥ kuśanābhaśca catvāro gādhirāsīt kuśāmbujaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: From him (Ajaka) was born Kuśa who had four sons viz. Kuśāmbu, Tanaya (v.l. Mūrtaya), Vasu and Kuśanābha. Kuśāmbu’s son was Gādhi.

devanagari: तस्य सत्यवतीं कन्यां ऋचीकोऽयाचत द्विजः । वरं विसदृशं मत्वा गाधिर्भार्गवमब्रवीत् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya satyavatīṃ kanyāṃ ṛcīko varaṃ visadṛśaṃ matvā gādhirbhārgavamabravīt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: A Brāhmaṇa called Ṛcīka sought Gādhi’s daughter, princess Satyavatī in marriage. Considering that the bridegroom was unworthy of her, king Gādhi spoke to Ṛcīka of the Bhṛgu clan:

devanagari: एकतः श्यामकर्णानां हयानां चन्द्रवर्चसाम् । सहस्रं दीयतां शुल्कं कन्यायाः कुशिका वयम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: ekataḥ śyāmakarṇānāṃ hayānāṃ candravarcasām sahasraṃ dīyatāṃ śulkaṃ kanyāyāḥ kuśikā vayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single], [imperative passive third single]

translation: “We belong to the race of Kuśa, and as such one thousand horses white and bright like the moon, but with one black ear be given as the dowry for the girl.”

devanagari: इत्युक्तस्तन्मतं ज्ञात्वा गतः स वरुणान्तिकम् । आनीय दत्त्वा तान् अश्वान् उपयेमे वराननाम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktastanmataṃ jñātvā gataḥ sa varuṇāntikam ānīya dattvā tān aśvān upayeme varānanām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: When he was thus replied, he understood the intention of Gādhi. Having approached Varuṇa, he brought and delivered the required horses of the said description and married that lovely princess.

devanagari: स ऋषिः प्रार्थितः पत्‍न्या श्वश्र्वा चापत्यकाम्यया । श्रपयित्वोभयैर्मन्त्रैः चरुं स्नातुं गतो मुनिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ṛṣiḥ prārthitaḥ pat‍nyā śvaśrvā cāpatyakāmyayā śrapayitvobhayairmantraiḥ caruṃ snātuṃ gato muniḥ
translation: The sage Ṛcīka was solicited by his wife and by his mother-in-law both of whom were desirous of having a son. He prepared Caru—an oblation of rice, barley and pulse boiled in butter and milk for presentations to gods—consecrated them severally—the one, for his wife, with Brāhma mantras, and the other, for his mother-in-law, consecrated with Kṣātra mantras. And then the sage went for having his bath.

devanagari: तावत् सत्यवती मात्रा स्वचरुं याचिता सती । श्रेष्ठं मत्वा तयायच्छन् मात्रे मातुरदत् स्वयम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvat satyavatī mātrā svacaruṃ yācitā satī śreṣṭhaṃ matvā tayāyacchan mātre māturadat svayam
translation: In the meanwhile (during his absence), Satyavatī, was requested by her mother (to give her) the Caru (sacrificial boiled rice) meant for Satyavatī herself, considering that to be superior. She gave that Caru (meant for herself) to her mother, and ate the one apportioned for her mother.

devanagari: तद् विज्ञाय मुनिः प्राह पत्‍नीं कष्टमकारषीः । घोरो दण्डधरः पुत्रो भ्राता ते ब्रह्मवित्तमः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tad vijñāya muniḥ prāha pat‍nīṃ kaṣṭamakāraṣīḥ ghoro daṇḍadharaḥ putro bhrātā te brahmavittamaḥ
translation: On learning of this (exchange of Carus), the sage told his wife, “Alas! What grievous mistake you have committed! Your son will be a terrible chastiser (like god of death), and your brother will be a prominent knower of Brahman (the Vedas or the Supreme Deity).

devanagari: प्रसादितः सत्यवत्या मैवं भूरिति भार्गवः । अथ तर्हि भवेत् पौत्रो जमदग्निस्ततोऽभवत् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: prasāditaḥ satyavatyā maivaṃ bhūriti bhārgavaḥ atha tarhi bhavet pautro jamadagnistato
translation: Propitiated by persuasive prayer that such eventuality should not materialise, sage Ṛcīka of the Bhṛgu clan said, “If so, your grandson will be of that nature”. Then Jamadagni was born.

devanagari: सा चाभूत् सुमहपुण्या कौशिकी लोकपावनी । रेणोः सुतां रेणुकां वै जमदग्निरुवाह याम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: sā cābhūt sumahapuṇyā kauśikī lokapāvanī reṇoḥ sutāṃ reṇukāṃ vai jamadagniruvāha yām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Then Satyavatī was transformed into a very sacred river Kauśikī, the sanctifier of the world. The tradition goes that Jamadagni married Reṇukā, the daughter of (King) Reṇu.

devanagari: तस्यां वै भार्गवऋषेः सुता वसुमदादयः । यवीयान्जज्ञ एतेषां राम इत्यभिविश्रुतः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ vai bhārgavaṛṣeḥ sutā vasumadādayaḥ yavīyānjajña eteṣāṃ rāma ityabhiviśrutaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: They say that several sons like Vasumat and others were begotten on her by the sage Jamadagni of the Bhṛgu clan. The youngest of them born was the great celebrity Rāma (Paraśurāma).

devanagari: यमाहुर्वासुदेवांशं हैहयानां कुलान्तकम् । त्रिःसप्तकृत्वो य इमां चक्रे निःक्षत्रियां महीम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: yamāhurvāsudevāṃśaṃ haihayānāṃ kulāntakam triḥsaptakṛtvo ya imāṃ cakre niḥkṣatriyāṃ mahīm

translation: [1] They say that he was a ray (aṃśa) of Vāsudeva (Lord Viṣṇu) and the exterminator of the Haihaya clan. He is said to have rendered this earth devoid of Kṣatriyas twenty-one times.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The Bhāgavata Purāṇa contains two main legends from the life of Paraśurāma viz. 1. The slaying of Kārtavīrya or Sahasrārjuna and 2. Beheading his mother and brothers and reviving them.
The legends of Paraśurāma narrated here are found in Mahābhārata Ādi. 2.4-7 64.4; Āśvamedhika chs. 29 & 30 and in Brahmāṇḍa P. II.26-40.

devanagari: दुष्टं क्षत्रं भुवो भारं अब्रह्मण्यं अनीनशत् । रजस्तमोवृतमहन् फल्गुन्यपि कृतेंऽहसि ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: duṣṭaṃ kṣatraṃ bhuvo bhāraṃ abrahmaṇyaṃ anīnaśat rajastamovṛtamahan phalgunyapi kṛteṃ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: He destroyed the wicked Kṣatriya race turned inimical to Brāhmaṇas, and constituted as if a burden to the earth. Even on the slightest provocation or commission of a slight offence, he killed the Kṣattriyas enveloped by (i.e. full of) rajas and tamas.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । किं तदंहो भगवतो राजन्यैरजितात्मभिः । कृतं येन कुलं नष्टं क्षत्रियाणां अभीक्ष्णशः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca kiṃ tadaṃho bhagavato rājanyairajitātmabhiḥ kṛtaṃ yena kulaṃ naṣṭaṃ kṣatriyāṇāṃ abhīkṣṇaśaḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The king (Parīkṣit) asked:
What offence against that glorious sage (Paraśurāma) was committed by Kṣatriyas of uncontrolled nature that led to the repeated extermination of the entire Kṣatriya race (by him)?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । हैहयानां अधिपतिः अर्जुनः क्षत्रियर्षभः । दत्तं नारायण अंशांशं आराध्य परिकर्मभिः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca haihayānāṃ adhipatiḥ arjunaḥ kṣatriyarṣabhaḥ dattaṃ nārāyaṇa aṃśāṃśaṃ ārādhya parikarmabhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka narrated:
Arjuna, a prominent Kṣatriya and the ruler of Haihayas (a branch of Yādavas) propitiated Datta (Dattātreya), a partial (aṃśa) incarnation of Lord Nārāyaṇa, by rendering to him various acts of service (and worship).

devanagari: बाहून् दशशतं लेभे दुर्धर्षत्वमरातिषु । अव्याहतेन्द्रियौजः श्री तेजोवीर्ययशोबलम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: bāhūn daśaśataṃ lebhe durdharṣatvamarātiṣu avyāhatendriyaujaḥ śrī tejovīryayaśobalam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Through him (Datta’s grace), he secured a thousand arms, unassailable formidability against the enemies, uninterrupted and insuperable power of senses and organs, splendour, affluence, glory, prowess, fame, physical strength, mastery over yoga including supreme spiritual powers characterised by the capacity to assume atomic size etc. His movements and speed being thus unimpeded, he moved about the world free as a wind.

devanagari: योगेश्वरत्वं ऐश्वर्यं गुणा यत्राणिमादयः । चचार अव्याहतगतिः लोकेषु पवनो यथा ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yogeśvaratvaṃ aiśvaryaṃ guṇā yatrāṇimādayaḥ cacāra avyāhatagatiḥ lokeṣu pavano yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Through him (Datta’s grace), he secured a thousand arms, unassailable formidability against the enemies, uninterrupted and insuperable power of senses and organs, splendour, affluence, glory, prowess, fame, physical strength, mastery over yoga including supreme spiritual powers characterised by the capacity to assume atomic size etc. His movements and speed being thus unimpeded, he moved about the world free as a wind.

devanagari: स्त्रीरत्‍नैः आवृतः क्रीडन् रेवाम्भसि मदोत्कटः । वैजयन्तीं स्रजं बिभ्रद् रुरोध सरितं भुजैः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: strīrat‍naiḥ āvṛtaḥ krīḍan revāmbhasi madotkaṭaḥ vaijayantīṃ srajaṃ bibhrad rurodha saritaṃ bhujaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: While sporting in the waters of the Narmadā, in company of (lit. surrounded by) jewel-like (charming) women, and wearing the Vaijayantī wreath (of nine well-known gems) he, being intoxicated with pride, obstructed the course of the river with his one thousand arms.

devanagari: विप्लावितं स्वशिबिरं प्रतिस्रोतःसरिज्जलैः । नामृष्यत् तस्य तद् वीर्यं वीरमानी दशाननः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: viplāvitaṃ svaśibiraṃ pratisrotaḥsarijjalaiḥ nāmṛṣyat tasya tad vīryaṃ vīramānī daśānanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ vīr class 10 verb]

translation: (Rāvaṇa was on his expedition of the world conquest and was camping on the bank of the Narmadā, near Māhiṣmatī, Sahasrārjuna’s capital and was then engaged in worship). Finding his encampment flooded with the waters of the river flowing in the opposite direction, Rāvaṇa, the tenheaded demon, who regarded himself as the only hero in the world, did not tolerate that (display of) prowess (by Sahasrārjuna).

devanagari: गृहीतो लीलया स्त्रीणां समक्षं कृतकिल्बिषः । माहिष्मत्यां सन्निरुद्धो मुक्तो येन कपिर्यथा ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhīto līlayā strīṇāṃ samakṣaṃ kṛtakilbiṣaḥ māhiṣmatyāṃ sanniruddho mukto yena kapiryathā
translation: When Rāvaṇa (tried to attack him during the water-sports and thus) offended Sahasrārjuna, he (Rāvaṇa) was sportively seized in the very presence of women, and was kept captive like a monkey at his capital Māhiṣmatī and was (ultimately) let off as a worthless fellow.

devanagari: स एकदा तु मृगयां विचरन् विजने वने । यदृच्छयाऽश्रमपदं जमदग्नेरुपाविशत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ekadā tu mṛgayāṃ vicaran vijane vane yadṛcchayā
translation: On one occasion, while Arjuna was on a hunting expedition in an impassable forest, he perchance, happened to come to the precincts of the hermitage of the sage Jamadagni.

devanagari: तस्मै स नरदेवाय मुनिरर्हणमाहरत् । ससैन्यामात्यवाहाय हविष्मत्या तपोधनः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmai sa naradevāya munirarhaṇamāharat sasainyāmātyavāhāya haviṣmatyā tapodhanaḥ
translation: Through the grace of his Kāmadhenu (wish-yielding cow), the sage whose wealth was (nothing but his) penance, however, offered a warm reception (with due formalities) to the king, his ministers, men in his army and even the animals that carried them.

devanagari: स वै रत्‍नं तु तद् दृष्ट्वा आत्मैश्वर्यातिशायनम् । तन्नाद्रियताग्निहोत्र्यां साभिलाषः स हैहयः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai rat‍naṃ tu tad dṛṣṭvā ātmaiśvaryātiśāyanam tannādriyatāgnihotryāṃ sābhilāṣaḥ sa haihayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Finding the affluence of the sage far surpassing his own, the warrior who was accompanied by his Haihaya clansmen, cherished covetousness for the wish-yielding cow which was essential for maintaining sacrificial fires (agnihotra), and did not appreciate the warm hospitality.

devanagari: हविर्धानीं ऋषेर्दर्पान् नरान् हर्तुमचोदयत् । ते च माहिष्मतीं निन्युः सवत्सां क्रन्दतीं बलात् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: havirdhānīṃ ṛṣerdarpān narān hartumacodayat te ca māhiṣmatīṃ ninyuḥ savatsāṃ krandatīṃ balāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ krand class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ krand class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ krand class 1 verb], [locative single from √ krand class 1 verb]

translation: Out of arrogance and pride, he commanded his men to take away the sage’s cow of plenty (which yielded milk, ghee etc. as oblations to sacrificial fires), and they forcibly carried off the screaming cow and her calf to (the king’s capital) Māhiṣmatī.

devanagari: अथ राजनि निर्याते राम आश्रम आगतः । श्रुत्वा तत् तस्य दौरात्म्यं चुक्रोधाहिरिवाहतः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: atha rājani niryāte rāma āśrama āgataḥ śrutvā tat tasya daurātmyaṃ cukrodhāhirivāhataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: After the king’s departure, (Paraśu) -Rāma arrived at the hermitage. Being informed of the act of wickedness of the king, he got enraged like a serpent that is hit.

devanagari: घोरमादाय परशुं सतूणं चर्म कार्मुकम् । अन्वधावत दुर्धर्षो मृगेन्द्र इव यूथपम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: ghoramādāya paraśuṃ satūṇaṃ carma kārmukam anvadhāvata durdharṣo mṛgendra iva yūthapam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Equipping himself with his formidable axe, a bow with quivers and a shield, the irresistible hero rushed after him like a lion chasing a king elephant of a herd.

devanagari: तं आपतन्तं भृगुवर्यमोजसा । धनुर्धरं बाणपरश्वधायुधम् । ऐणेयचर्माम्बरमर्कधामभिः । युतं जटाभिर्ददृशे पुरीं विशन् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ āpatantaṃ bhṛguvaryamojasā dhanurdharaṃ bāṇaparaśvadhāyudham aiṇeyacarmāmbaramarkadhāmabhiḥ yutaṃ jaṭābhirdadṛśe purīṃ viśan

[nominative single from √ yu class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ viś class 6 verb]

translation: As Arjuna was entering his capital, he noticed the prominent Bhṛgu, Paraśurāma bearing a bow and armed with arrows and an axe, wearing a black deer-skin and with matted hair resplendent like the sun, rushing (after him) with an irresistible force.

devanagari: अचोदयद्धस्तिरथाश्वपत्तिभिः । गदासिबाणर्ष्टिशतघ्निशक्तिभिः । अक्षौहिणीः सप्तदशातिभीषणाः । ता राम एको भगवानसूदयत् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: acodayaddhastirathāśvapattibhiḥ gadāsibāṇarṣṭiśataghniśaktibhiḥ akṣauhiṇīḥ saptadaśātibhīṣaṇāḥ tā rāma eko bhagavānasūdayat
translation: (To oppose him) Arjuna directed seventeen very formidable divisions (akṣauhiṇī)[2] consisting of elephants, chariots, cavalry and infantry equipped with maces, swords, arrows, javelins, Śataghnīs (a rocket-like missile or a cylindrical block of wood studded with iron pikes) and darts or lances (Śaktis). But the glorious Lord Rāma singlehandedly slaughtered them all.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
For the details of the strength of an akṣauhiṇī vide Bhāgavata Purāṇa VII.I. P. 71 ftn. No. 271.

devanagari: यतो यतोऽसौ प्रहरत्परश्वधो । मनोऽनिलौजाः परचक्रसूदनः । ततस्ततश्छिन्नभुजोरुकन्धरा । निपेतुरुर्व्यां हतसूतवाहनाः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: yato yato mano tatastataśchinnabhujorukandharā nipetururvyāṃ hatasūtavāhanāḥ

translation: In whatever direction, Rāma, the destroyer of hostile forces, moved with the velocity of the wind and mind, striking down with his axe, there fell down on the ground enemies with their arms, thighs and necks chopped off and their charioteers and animals slain.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा स्वसैन्यं रुधिरौघकर्दमे । रणाजिरे रामकुठारसायकैः । विवृक्णचर्मध्वजचापविग्रहं । निपातितं हैहय आपतद् रुषा ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā svasainyaṃ rudhiraughakardame raṇājire rāmakuṭhārasāyakaiḥ vivṛkṇacarmadhvajacāpavigrahaṃ nipātitaṃ haihaya āpatad ruṣā
translation: Perceiving that his army is laid low on the battlefield in the mire caused by streams of blood, with their shields, flags, bows and bodies minced down by the axe and shafts of Rāma, the Haihaya King Arjuna, rushed forward in rage.

devanagari: अथार्जुनः पञ्चशतेषु बाहुभिः । धनुःषु बाणान् युगपत् स सन्दधे । रामाय रामोऽस्त्रभृतां समग्रणीः । तान्येकधन्वेषुभिराच्छिनत् समम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: athārjunaḥ pañcaśateṣu bāhubhiḥ dhanuḥṣu bāṇān yugapat sa sandadhe rāmāya rāmo tānyekadhanveṣubhirācchinat samam

translation: Now Arjuna (being thousand-armed) simultaneously fitted shafts on five hundred bows in order to discharge them at Rāma. But Rāma, the undisputed leader of all whoever wielded missiles, with one bow in his hand at once—synchronously—cut down all Arjuna’s bow and arrows with his shafts.

devanagari: पुनः स्वहस्तैरचलान्मृधेङ्घ्रिपान् । उत्क्षिप्य वेगाद् अभिधावतो युधि । भुजान् कुठारेण कठोरनेमिना । चिच्छेद रामः प्रसभं त्वहेरिव ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: punaḥ svahastairacalānmṛdheṅghripān utkṣipya vegād abhidhāvato yudhi bhujān kuṭhāreṇa kaṭhoraneminā ciccheda rāmaḥ prasabhaṃ tvaheriva

translation: In that fight, while Arjuna was rushing towards Rāma uprooting rocks and trees with his (numerous) hands (to hurl them at Rāma), Rāma forestalled him and quickly cut down his arms like the hoods of serpents, with his axe of a hard and sharp edge.

devanagari: कृत्तबाहोः शिरस्तस्य गिरेः श्रृङमिवाहरत् । हते पितरि तत्पुत्रा अयुतं दुद्रुवुर्भयात् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛttabāhoḥ śirastasya gireḥ śrṛṅamivāharat hate pitari tatputrā ayutaṃ dudruvurbhayāt

translation: After lopping off all his arms, Rāma chopped off Arjuna’s head like the peak of a mountain. When their father was thus killed, Arjuna’s ten thousand sons ran away in panic.

devanagari: अग्निहोत्रीं उपावर्त्य सवत्सां परवीरहा । समुपेत्याश्रमं पित्रे परिक्लिष्टां समर्पयत् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: agnihotrīṃ upāvartya savatsāṃ paravīrahā samupetyāśramaṃ pitre parikliṣṭāṃ samarpayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ṛ ], [vocative single from √ ṛ ], [accusative single from √ ṛ ]

translation: Rāma, the subduer of hostile forces, restored the cow (essential for the maintenance of Agnihotra) along with her calf. Returning to his hermitage, he duly presented the afflicted cow to his father.

devanagari: स्वकर्म तत्कृतं रामः पित्रे भ्रातृभ्य एव च । वर्णयामास तत् श्रुत्वा जमदग्निरभाषत ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: svakarma tatkṛtaṃ rāmaḥ pitre bhrātṛbhya eva ca varṇayāmāsa tat śrutvā jamadagnirabhāṣata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Rāma, described to his father and brothers (Arjuna’s moves) and his exploits; but the sage Jamadagni (disapprovingly) remarked:

devanagari: राम राम महाबाहो भवान् पापमकारषीत् । अवधीत् नरदेवं यत् सर्वदेवमयं वृथा ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: rāma rāma mahābāho bhavān pāpamakāraṣīt avadhīt naradevaṃ yat sarvadevamayaṃ vṛthā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: “(Dear) Rāma! Oh Rāma of mighty arms! You have committed a sin. You have unnecessarily killed a king (a god among men) who represents all gods in his person.

devanagari: वयं हि ब्राह्मणास्तात क्षमयार्हणतां गताः । यया लोकगुरुर्देवः पारमेष्ठ्यमगात् पदम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: vayaṃ hi brāhmaṇāstāta kṣamayārhaṇatāṃ gatāḥ yayā lokagururdevaḥ pārameṣṭhyamagāt padam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: Oh dear child! We are Brāhmaṇas! We have become objects of veneration and worship through forbearance, just as god Brahmā, the creator of the world, has attained to the status of Parameṣṭhī (the supreme ruler of the world) through forgiveness.

devanagari: क्षमया रोचते लक्ष्मीः ब्राह्मी सौरी यथा प्रभा । क्षमिणामाशु भगवान् तुष्यते हरिरीश्वरः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: kṣamayā rocate lakṣmīḥ brāhmī saurī yathā prabhā kṣamiṇāmāśu bhagavān tuṣyate harirīśvaraḥ
translation: It is on account of forgiveness and patience that the Brāhmanic glory shines like the splendour of the sun. Even glorious Lord Hari, the Ruler of the Universe, become seasily pleased with persons of forgiving spirits.

devanagari: राज्ञो मूर्धाभिषिक्तस्य वधो ब्रह्मवधाद् गुरुः । तीर्थसंसेवया चांहो जह्यङ्गाच्युतचेतनः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: rājño mūrdhābhiṣiktasya vadho brahmavadhād guruḥ tīrthasaṃsevayā cāṃho jahyaṅgācyutacetanaḥ
translation:
Slaying of a king whose head is consecrated in coronation, is more heinous than that of killing a Brāhmaṇa. Oh child! Now expiate your sin by bathing in holy waters, devoting your mind (all the while) to Lord Viṣṇu (Acyuta).”
